  "text": "The G1 GC concurrent marking and mixed collection cycle in openjdk/jdk is implemented across several C++ files under src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/.\n\n## 1. G1ConcurrentMark Start Trigger\n\nThe trigger logic lives in `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1Policy.cpp`:\n- `G1Policy::need_to_start_conc_mark()`: Evaluates heap occupancy against the InitiatingHeapOccupancyPercent threshold (IHOP) to decide whether a concurrent mark cycle is necessary.\n- `G1Policy::maybe_start_marking()`: Called at the end of each young GC; if `need_to_start_conc_mark()` returns true, it sets the `initiate_conc_mark_if_possible` flag on the collector state.\n- `G1Policy::initiate_conc_mark()`: Finalizes the decision and sets the collector into concurrent-mark-start mode, clearing the `initiate_conc_mark_if_possible` flag.\n\nThe `G1ConcurrentMarkThread` (in `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1ConcurrentMarkThread.cpp`) runs the actual cycle:\n- `G1ConcurrentMarkThread::run_service()`: Main service loop calling `wait_for_next_cycle()` then `concurrent_mark_cycle_do()`.\n- `G1ConcurrentMarkThread::wait_for_next_cycle()`: Blocks until the policy triggers a new cycle.\n- `G1ConcurrentMarkThread::concurrent_mark_cycle_do()`: Orchestrates the full marking cycle: scan root regions, mark from roots, preclean, remark, rebuild/scrub, cleanup, and clear bitmap.\n\nThe `G1ConcurrentMark` class (in `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1ConcurrentMark.cpp`) provides:\n- `G1ConcurrentMark::pre_concurrent_start()`: Prepares the mark cycle before it begins.\n- `G1ConcurrentMark::concurrent_cycle_start()`: Records the cycle start.\n- `G1ConcurrentMark::scan_root_regions()`: Scans root memory regions to populate the initial mark worklist.\n- `G1ConcurrentMark::mark_from_roots()`: Drives the concurrent marking workers.\n- `G1ConcurrentMark::remark()`: Stop-the-world remark phase.\n- `G1ConcurrentMark::cleanup()`: Post-marking cleanup, triggering collection set candidate computation.\n\n## 2. SATB Marking Queue Processing\n\nThe SATB (Snapshot-At-The-Beginning) write barrier queues are managed by:\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1SATBMarkQueueSet.cpp`: `G1SATBMarkQueueSet::filter()` removes already-marked or dead objects from a thread-local SATB buffer before flushing. `G1SATBMarkQueueSet::handle_zero_index_for_thread()` handles buffer overflow.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/shared/satbMarkQueue.cpp`: Base class `SATBMarkQueueSet::apply_closure_to_completed_buffer()` applies a marking closure to a completed buffer popped from the global queue; `SATBMarkQueueSet::flush_queue()` flushes a thread-local queue to the global set.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1ConcurrentMark.cpp`: `G1CMTask::drain_satb_buffers()` is called during concurrent marking and remark to drain all pending SATB buffers, applying the marking closure to each entry.\n\n## 3. Remembered Set Scanning\n\nRemembered set scanning is implemented in `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1RemSet.cpp`:\n- `G1RemSet::prepare_for_scan_heap_roots()`: Initializes scan state.\n- `G1RemSet::merge_heap_roots()`: Merges card table entries from all remembered sets of collection set regions into the scan worklist (the `G1MergeHeapRootsTask` inner class does the heavy lifting, including the `G1ScanHRForRegionClosure`).\n- `G1RemSet::scan_heap_roots()`: Per-worker function that scans the merged heap root cards, evacuating live objects referenced from outside the collection set.\n- `G1RemSet::scan_collection_set_optional_roots()`: Scans optional collection set regions' remembered sets.\n- `G1RemSet::scan_collection_set_code_roots()`: Scans code roots referencing collection set regions.\n- `G1RemSet::refine_card_concurrently()`: Concurrent refinement of dirty cards into the remembered set structures.\n\n## 4. Mixed Collection Region Selection\n\nAfter marking completes, candidate old regions are selected for mixed GC:\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1CollectionSetChooser.cpp`: `G1CollectionSetChooser::build()` parallelizes scanning of all old regions, filters those with low enough occupancy (`region_occupancy_low_enough_for_evac()`), sorts them by reclaimable bytes descending, and populates the `G1CollectionSetCandidates` structure.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1CollectionSetCandidates.cpp` / `.hpp`: Holds the sorted list of marking candidates (from the concurrent mark) and retained candidates (pinned regions from prior mixed GCs).\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1CollectionSet.cpp`:\n  - `G1CollectionSet::select_candidates_from_marking()`: During mixed GC phases (`in_mixed_phase()`), picks old regions from the marking candidate list up to min/max old-CSet length bounds and time budget.\n  - `G1CollectionSet::select_candidates_from_retained()`: Adds any retained (previously pinned) candidate regions.\n  - `G1CollectionSet::select_candidates_from_optional_groups()`: Optionally adds more regions within remaining time budget.\n  - `G1CollectionSet::add_old_region()`: Adds a selected old region to the final collection set."
}

## G1 GC Concurrent Marking and Mixed Collection Cycle

### 1. Concurrent Mark Start Trigger
- **`g1Policy.cpp`**: `G1Policy::need_to_start_conc_mark()` checks heap occupancy vs IHOP threshold; `G1Policy::maybe_start_marking()` sets the trigger flag after each young GC; `G1Policy::initiate_conc_mark()` finalizes the decision.
- **`g1ConcurrentMarkThread.cpp`**: `G1ConcurrentMarkThread::run_service()` is the main loop; `wait_for_next_cycle()` waits for the policy trigger; `concurrent_mark_cycle_do()` orchestrates the full cycle.
- **`g1ConcurrentMark.cpp`**: `G1ConcurrentMark::pre_concurrent_start()` and `concurrent_cycle_start()` initialize the mark cycle.

### 2. SATB Queue Processing
- **`g1SATBMarkQueueSet.cpp`**: `G1SATBMarkQueueSet::filter()` prunes dead/already-marked objects from thread-local buffers; `handle_zero_index_for_thread()` handles buffer overflow.
- **`satbMarkQueue.cpp`** (shared): `SATBMarkQueueSet::apply_closure_to_completed_buffer()` processes completed buffers; `flush_queue()` pushes thread-local buffers to the global set.
- **`g1ConcurrentMark.cpp`**: `G1CMTask::drain_satb_buffers()` drains all SATB queues during marking and remark.

### 3. Remembered Set Scanning
- **`g1RemSet.cpp`**: `G1RemSet::merge_heap_roots()` merges remembered set card entries into the scan worklist; `scan_heap_roots()` does per-worker scanning; `scan_collection_set_optional_roots()` handles optional regions.

### 4. Mixed Collection Region Selection
- **`g1CollectionSetChooser.cpp`**: `G1CollectionSetChooser::build()` parallelizes collection of old region candidates, sorted by reclaimable bytes.
- **`g1CollectionSetCandidates.cpp`**: Holds marking and retained candidate lists.
- **`g1CollectionSet.cpp`**: `select_candidates_from_marking()` picks old regions during mixed GC phases up to time/count budgets.

  "text": "The G1 GC concurrent marking and mixed collection cycle in openjdk/jdk is implemented across several C++ files under src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/.\n\n## 1. G1ConcurrentMark Start Trigger\n\nThe trigger logic lives in `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1Policy.cpp`:\n- `G1Policy::need_to_start_conc_mark()`: Evaluates heap occupancy against the InitiatingHeapOccupancyPercent threshold (IHOP) to decide whether a concurrent mark cycle is necessary.\n- `G1Policy::maybe_start_marking()`: Called at the end of each young GC; if `need_to_start_conc_mark()` returns true, it sets the `initiate_conc_mark_if_possible` flag on the collector state.\n- `G1Policy::initiate_conc_mark()`: Finalizes the decision and sets the collector into concurrent-mark-start mode, clearing the `initiate_conc_mark_if_possible` flag.\n\nThe `G1ConcurrentMarkThread` (in `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1ConcurrentMarkThread.cpp`) runs the actual cycle:\n- `G1ConcurrentMarkThread::run_service()`: Main service loop calling `wait_for_next_cycle()` then `concurrent_mark_cycle_do()`.\n- `G1ConcurrentMarkThread::wait_for_next_cycle()`: Blocks until the policy triggers a new cycle.\n- `G1ConcurrentMarkThread::concurrent_mark_cycle_do()`: Orchestrates the full marking cycle: scan root regions, mark from roots, preclean, remark, rebuild/scrub, cleanup, and clear bitmap.\n\nThe `G1ConcurrentMark` class (in `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1ConcurrentMark.cpp`) provides:\n- `G1ConcurrentMark::pre_concurrent_start()`: Prepares the mark cycle before it begins.\n- `G1ConcurrentMark::concurrent_cycle_start()`: Records the cycle start.\n- `G1ConcurrentMark::scan_root_regions()`: Scans root memory regions to populate the initial mark worklist.\n- `G1ConcurrentMark::mark_from_roots()`: Drives the concurrent marking workers.\n- `G1ConcurrentMark::remark()`: Stop-the-world remark phase.\n- `G1ConcurrentMark::cleanup()`: Post-marking cleanup, triggering collection set candidate computation.\n\n## 2. SATB Marking Queue Processing\n\nThe SATB (Snapshot-At-The-Beginning) write barrier queues are managed by:\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1SATBMarkQueueSet.cpp`: `G1SATBMarkQueueSet::filter()` removes already-marked or dead objects from a thread-local SATB buffer before flushing. `G1SATBMarkQueueSet::handle_zero_index_for_thread()` handles buffer overflow.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/shared/satbMarkQueue.cpp`: Base class `SATBMarkQueueSet::apply_closure_to_completed_buffer()` applies a marking closure to a completed buffer popped from the global queue; `SATBMarkQueueSet::flush_queue()` flushes a thread-local queue to the global set.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1ConcurrentMark.cpp`: `G1CMTask::drain_satb_buffers()` is called during concurrent marking and remark to drain all pending SATB buffers, applying the marking closure to each entry.\n\n## 3. Remembered Set Scanning\n\nRemembered set scanning is implemented in `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1RemSet.cpp`:\n- `G1RemSet::prepare_for_scan_heap_roots()`: Initializes scan state.\n- `G1RemSet::merge_heap_roots()`: Merges card table entries from all remembered sets of collection set regions into the scan worklist (the `G1MergeHeapRootsTask` inner class does the heavy lifting, including the `G1ScanHRForRegionClosure`).\n- `G1RemSet::scan_heap_roots()`: Per-worker function that scans the merged heap root cards, evacuating live objects referenced from outside the collection set.\n- `G1RemSet::scan_collection_set_optional_roots()`: Scans optional collection set regions' remembered sets.\n- `G1RemSet::scan_collection_set_code_roots()`: Scans code roots referencing collection set regions.\n- `G1RemSet::refine_card_concurrently()`: Concurrent refinement of dirty cards into the remembered set structures.\n\n## 4. Mixed Collection Region Selection\n\nAfter marking completes, candidate old regions are selected for mixed GC:\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1CollectionSetChooser.cpp`: `G1CollectionSetChooser::build()` parallelizes scanning of all old regions, filters those with low enough occupancy (`region_occupancy_low_enough_for_evac()`), sorts them by reclaimable bytes descending, and populates the `G1CollectionSetCandidates` structure.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1CollectionSetCandidates.cpp` / `.hpp`: Holds the sorted list of marking candidates (from the concurrent mark) and retained candidates (pinned regions from prior mixed GCs).\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1CollectionSet.cpp`:\n  - `G1CollectionSet::select_candidates_from_marking()`: During mixed GC phases (`in_mixed_phase()`), picks old regions from the marking candidate list up to min/max old-CSet length bounds and time budget.\n  - `G1CollectionSet::select_candidates_from_retained()`: Adds any retained (previously pinned) candidate regions.\n  - `G1CollectionSet::select_candidates_from_optional_groups()`: Optionally adds more regions within remaining time budget.\n  - `G1CollectionSet::add_old_region()`: Adds a selected old region to the final collection set."
}
